pr.title: Match grInishie1_801FA9B4 to 100% by copying the same structure for StageData found in grHeal
pr.author: DavidWeaverAudio

summary: Single-file stage decomp matching PR for Inishie1. It matched `grInishie1_801FA9B4` to 100% by modeling the surrounding `.data` layout: a preceding s16/S16Vec3 table, StageCallbacks adjacency, a StageData wrapper with an embedded get-gobj report format string, and separate global strings for `/GrI1.dat` and `grinishie1.c`.

key_files.reason: Only changed file; added Inishie1 stage data globals and adjusted `grInishie1_801FA9B4` to reference that data layout.

what_changed: Added `s16 grI1_803E48C8[22]` and used it in StageData as `(S16Vec3*) grI1_803E48C8` with count `7`.

Added `typedef struct grInishie1_StageData { StageData stage_data; char report_fmt_get_gobj[0x24]; }` and initialized `grI1_803E4950` with `INISHIE1`, the `/GrI1.dat` path, the S16Vec3 table pointer, and `"%s:%d: couldn t get gobj(id=%d)\n"`.
Changed `grInishie1_801FA9B4` callback lookup from direct `&grI1_803E48F4[arg0]` to `(StageCallbacks*) ((char*) grI1_803E48C8 + 0x2c) + arg0`, tying codegen to the preceding data block.
Changed the failed `Ground_GetStageGObj` OSReport call to use `grI1_803E4950.report_fmt_get_gobj` and `grI1_803E49A8` instead of inline string literals.

decomp.dev reported one new match: `main/melee/gr/grinishie1` item `grInishie1_801FA9B4` improved from 94.03% to 100.00%; `.data` improved from 19.85% to 55.52%; overall matched code increased by 236 bytes.
smart_moves: Identified the mismatch as a data-layout/symbol-reference problem rather than a control-flow rewrite problem.
Reused an existing stage-module pattern from `grHeal`: a StageData-containing wrapper followed by `report_fmt_get_gobj[0x24]`.
Used the fact that `grI1_803E48C8[22]` is 44 bytes (`0x2c`) to derive the adjacent `StageCallbacks` address in the form needed for matching.
Lifted report strings into globals/data fields so `OSReport` references matched the original data section instead of emitting direct local string references.
decomp_lessons: For stage get-gobj helpers, the nearby `.data` structure can be necessary to match a small function even when the C logic is already understood.
If direct `&callbacks[arg]` does not match, check whether the original code references an earlier data symbol plus a byte offset into an adjacent array.
Homologous stage files can provide reliable layout clues; here the PR title explicitly cites copying the StageData structure pattern from `grHeal`.
Wrapper structs with fixed-size trailing char arrays can be a practical way to model original data ordering for embedded format strings.
When casting raw `s16` tables to `S16Vec3*`, verify the byte size and count relationship; here 22 s16 entries produce the `0x2c` offset used to reach `grI1_803E48F4`.
naming_conventions: Inishie1 globals use the `grI1_803E....` address-style prefix, e.g. `grI1_803E48C8`, `grI1_803E48F4`, `grI1_803E4950`.
The local wrapper type follows module + purpose naming: `grInishie1_StageData`.
The embedded report format field is named descriptively as `report_fmt_get_gobj`, matching its use in the failed `Ground_GetStageGObj` path.
The stage data path string and source filename string are separate globals: `/GrI1.dat` in `grI1_803E4944` and `grinishie1.c` in `grI1_803E49A8`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used data adjacency explicitly: `(char*) grI1_803E48C8 + 0x2c` reaches the following `StageCallbacks grI1_803E48F4[]` because 22 s16 entries equal 44 bytes.
Replaced direct callback-array indexing with pointer arithmetic from the prior symbol to match the original address calculation.
Modeled the OSReport format string as part of a `StageData` wrapper struct so the function loads `grI1_803E4950.report_fmt_get_gobj`.
Separated declaration of `HSD_GObj* gobj` from the `Ground_GetStageGObj(arg0)` call in the function; the diff shows this ordering change, but the slice does not include an explicit rationale.
Used decomp.dev validation to confirm the tactic produced a 100% match for `grInishie1_801FA9B4`.
review_feedback: No human review comments or formal reviews were present in the slice.
decomp-dev[bot] reported GALE01 matched code at 69.35% with `+0.01%` and `+236 bytes`, including the new 100% match for `grInishie1_801FA9B4`.
follow_up_queries: Search other `src/melee/gr/*.c` files for direct `&StageCallbacks[arg]` uses that might need an earlier-data-symbol-plus-offset expression.
Search for inline `OSReport("%s:%d: couldn't get gobj` or get-gobj report strings that may need promotion into StageData-adjacent globals.
Compare other stage modules against `grHeal_StageData` and `grInishie1_StageData` for reusable `StageData + report_fmt_get_gobj[0x24]` layouts.
Check whether additional static size assertions would help document wrapper layouts; this PR did not add one for `grInishie1_StageData`.
